items.title: Syria, Palestinian Authority Join Regional Condemnation of Iranian Attacks on Kurdistan Region
items.desc: Syria and the Palestinian Authority have joined a widening regional response condemning Iran's attacks on the Kurdistan Region.

items.title: Prime Minister Barzani Urges Swift Formation of New KRG Cabinet, Warns Against Targeting Kurdistan Region
items.desc: Masrour Barzani inaugurates major drinking water project, calls on political parties to reopen parliament and put public interest first

items.title: Iraq, Syria Move to Restore Strategic Kirkuk-Baniyas Oil Pipeline
items.desc: The Iraq-Syria agreement to rehabilitate the Kirkuk-Baniyas pipeline could expand Iraqi export routes, restore Syria's role as an energy transit corridor, and strengthen regional energy connectivity.

items.title: Kurdistan Exports Onions Internationally for First Time, Marking Agricultural Milestone
items.desc: The first international shipment of 3,000 tons of Kurdistan-grown onions to Türkiye underscores the Region's broader strategy to expand agricultural exports, strengthen farmers' access to new markets, and diversify the economy.

items.title: KRG Expands Water Infrastructure as Ninth Cabinet Advances Long-Term Water Security
items.desc: Large-scale investments in potable water production, distribution networks, and wastewater treatment are reshaping the Kurdistan Region's long-term approach to water security and sustainable development.

items.title: Integrity Commission detains electricity official over $3.3M embezzlement
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq’s Federal Commission of Integrity on Sunday arrested a Baghdad electricity distribution official accused of embezzling electrical cables worth more than 4.4 billion Iraqi dinars (about $3.34M). An investigative team inspecting the Ministry of Electricity found that the warehouse manager at the Rusafa Distribution Branch had allegedly diverted the cables in coordinat

items.title: Syria’s Al-Sharaa appoints new intelligence chief
items.desc: Shafaq News- Damascus Syrian transitional President Ahmad Al-Sharaa on Sunday appointed new leaders to key security positions, including the National Security Office and the General Intelligence Service. According to Syria’s SANA news agency, under the decisions, Interior Minister Anas Khattab was named director of the National Security Office while retaining his post as interior minister. A

items.title: World Cup 2026 Final preview: Unstoppable Argentina vs. immovable Spain
items.desc: Shafaq News- New York/ New Jersey Spain and defending champions Argentina meet at last in the 2026 World Cup final on Sunday at New York New Jersey Stadium, with kickoff scheduled for 10:00 p.m. in Baghdad. items.title: US-Iran war costs Iraq 300M+ oil barrels
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq lost an estimated 302.8 million barrels of oil production during the first half of 2026 as the US-Iran war disrupted extraction and exports through the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Eco Iraq Observatory. The watchdog placed total production at about 440.3 million barrels between January and June. Daily volumes remained above 4.1 million barrels during the first two m

items.title: PM Al-Zaidi heads to Tehran with US-Iran dialogue proposal
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi intends to make an official visit to the Iranian capital, Tehran, at the end of this week, informed sources told Shafaq News on Sunday. Al-Zaidi will carry an Iraqi initiative aimed at contributing to an end to the current military confrontation between Iran and the United States and at opening channels for dialogue between the two sides. items.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
items.desc: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

items.title: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
items.desc: Iran and the US have traded strikes as hostilities re-escalated this week, with Tehran targeting US military facilities across the Gulf region.

items.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
items.desc: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

items.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
items.desc: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

items.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
items.desc: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

items.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
items.desc: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

items.title: Deadly overnight Russian attack on Ukraine targets the capital
items.desc: Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 attack drones across Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force.

items.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
items.desc: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

items.title: Josh Kerr of Britain breaks 27-year-old world record in the mile
items.desc: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.

items.title: U.S. launches new airstrikes to 'punish' Iran for troop deaths
items.desc: Two U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.

items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
items.desc: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

items.title: Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates
items.desc: There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback.

items.title: US strikes oil tanker with missiles as it enforces new Iran blockade
items.desc: The US military targeted an unladen oil tanker which it said was heading for the key Iranian oil terminal on Kharg island.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'
items.desc: US President Donald Trump says he is yet to decide whether or not he will "finish off" Iran.

items.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
items.desc: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.
